canonical_question: What does India's DPDP Act require — consent framework, data fiduciary obligations, cross-border rules?
aliases: Digital Personal Data Protection Act India
constraints: Digital personal data only — non-digital data outside scope
Phased enforcement: full compliance required by May 13, 2027
SDF obligations only for government-designated entities
Children threshold 18 years — verifiable parental consent required
Negative list not yet published — transfers currently unrestricted
Sector-specific laws (RBI, SEBI, IRDAI) may override DPDP cross-border provisions

skip_this_unit_if.condition: User is asking about EU data protection
User is asking about US privacy laws
User is asking about China PIPL
User is asking about Southeast Asian PDPA laws

often_confused_with.label: GDPR (different consent model, enforcement, cross-border mechanism)

inputs_needed.question: Is the entity a Data Fiduciary or Data Processor?
options: Data Fiduciary (determines purpose and means of processing)
Data Processor (processes on behalf of fiduciary)
Significant Data Fiduciary (designated by central government)

inputs_needed.question: Where does the entity process personal data of Indian individuals?
options: Outside India but offering goods/services to Indian individuals

inputs_needed.question: Does the entity process children's data (individuals under 18)?

applies_to.user_segment: Data fiduciaries processing personal data of Indian data principals
applies_to.context: Any entity processing digital personal data in India or processing abroad for offering goods/services in India
